decisions.summary: Boot recovery re-enters the SAME runForkPipeline (made resume-safe) rather than a duplicate recovery code path — one pipeline, no drift between live and recovery behavior.

Persist the full fork request as a JSON column (fork_request) on the fork conversation row at creation. The runForkPipeline params today live only in the dead process's memory; this is the minimal durable state needed to reconstruct the call after a restart.

Retry guard fork_retry_count capped at 2, incremented and persisted BEFORE each recovery attempt so a fork that crashes the dashboard during its own recovery still burns a retry and cannot crash-loop the server on every boot.

Recovery runs on a short delay after boot (scheduled setTimeout), not inline at the old clearStuckForks call site, so tmux/runtime-mirror/lifecycle services have settled enough to classify session state and avoid killing a working agent.

Legacy in-flight forks with no persisted fork_request fall back to the old behavior (mark failed). Only forks created after this change are recoverable; older ones degrade rather than guess parameters.

CLI honesty applies to BOTH consumers — handoff.ts and fork.ts — because they share forkConversationViaServer and contain the identical 'only check forkStatus===failed then print green' false-success bug.

Include the bounded graceful-shutdown grace window (the issue's 'cheap complement'): on SIGTERM/INT/HUP, await in-flight forks up to a cap before exit so the common pan-reload case never interrupts a fork.

No new config flags and no dashboard UI work. The change is confined to the server pipeline, the conversations DB schema, and the two CLI commands. This is a correctness/resilience bug, not a feature.

hazards.summary: tmux-alive-but-not-injected: a fork whose tmux session spawned but whose brief never landed must NOT be classified as 'completed' and marked active.
hazards.mitigation: The 'completed' dispatch branch requires BOTH the tmux session alive AND the runtime mirror reporting active/waiting-on-human (getAgentRuntimeStateSync). tmux-alive-but-idle re-enters the resumable pipeline to re-inject, it does not mark active.

hazards.summary: Double-authoring or double-spawn when the pipeline is re-entered during recovery.
hazards.mitigation: resumable-fork-pipeline bead makes runForkPipeline reuse an existing handoff_doc_path+file (skip authoring) and skip spawnConversationSession when the tmux session already exists (sessionExists).

hazards.summary: A fork that crashes the dashboard could send recovery into an infinite crash-loop on every boot.
hazards.mitigation: incrementForkRetryCount is called and persisted BEFORE each attempt; at retry_count >= 2 the fork is marked failed instead of re-attempted.

hazards.summary: clearStuckForks is currently called synchronously at boot and may be covered by an existing test; removing/repurposing it can break that test.
hazards.mitigation: Locate any clearStuckForks test; update or remove it as part of the boot-fork-recovery bead. Keep clearStuckForks only if still needed as a narrow terminal fail-marker, otherwise remove it and its callers/tests cleanly.

hazards.summary: Migration correctness: a non-additive or non-idempotent migration corrupts existing databases.
hazards.mitigation: Add columns to BOTH the CREATE TABLE block (fresh DBs) AND a try/catch ALTER TABLE migration step (existing DBs), bump SCHEMA_VERSION to 53, matching the established pattern in schema.ts. Verify running migrations twice does not throw.

hazards.summary: Graceful shutdown could block a restart indefinitely if an in-flight fork hangs.
hazards.mitigation: The shutdown wait is strictly bounded (~10s cap); a fork that does not settle in the window is logged and the process exits anyway. Boot recovery is the backstop for anything dropped.

hazards.summary: fork_request must contain every parameter runForkPipeline needs — an omitted field would make recovery re-enter with wrong/default behavior (silent wrong-fork).
hazards.mitigation: Define a single shared ForkRequest type enumerating all runForkPipeline args + parent conv name + sessionId; the persist bead serializes from exactly that type. This is the foundation bead's deep-inspection focus.

hazards.summary: Dashboard server is Node 22 only and must be rebuilt before restart; recovery/shutdown code runs in dist.
hazards.mitigation: After server changes, run npm run build before any restart; never run the server under Bun. (Project rule: dashboard-node22-only.)

sessionHistory.note: Planning session started for PAN-1744: Fork/handoff pipeline dies permanently on dashboard restart — resume in-flight forks on boot instead of failing them
